query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "minimax sparse attention" computer science
rationale: The paper investigates a fundamental limitation in LLMs regarding character-level understanding, which is a distinct construct from the user's specific focus on "Sparse Attention" mechanisms and the "MiniMax" architecture. It does not measure the same mechanism, provide a baseline for sparse attention efficiency, or belong to the canonical alternative-vocabulary cluster for attention optimization, thus failing all inclusion criteria (a-f).
